speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Fear me not, man. I will not break away.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I’ll give thee, ere I leave thee, so much money,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: To warrant thee, as I am ’rested for.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: My wife is in a wayward mood today

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And will not lightly trust the messenger

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: That I should be attached in Ephesus.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I tell you, ’twill sound harshly in her ears.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Here comes my man. I think he brings the money.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: How now, sir? Have you that I sent you for?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Here’s that, I warrant you, will pay them all.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: But where’s the money?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Why, sir, I gave the money for the rope.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Five hundred ducats, villain, for a rope?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I’ll serve you, sir, five hundred at the rate.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: To what end did I bid thee hie thee home?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: To a rope’s end, sir, and to that

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: end am I returned.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And to that end, sir, I will welcome you.

speaker: OFFICER
text: Good sir, be patient.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Nay, ’tis for me to be patient. I am

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: in adversity.

speaker: OFFICER
text: Good now, hold thy tongue.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Nay, rather persuade him to hold

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: his hands.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Thou whoreson, senseless

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: villain.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I would I were senseless, sir, that

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I might not feel your blows.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Thou art sensible in nothing

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: but blows, and so is an ass.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I am an ass, indeed; you may

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: prove it by my long ears.—I have served him from

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: the hour of my nativity to this instant, and have

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: nothing at his hands for my service but blows.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: When I am cold, he heats me with beating; when I

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: am warm, he cools me with beating. I am waked

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: with it when I sleep, raised with it when I sit,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: driven out of doors with it when I go from home,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: welcomed home with it when I return. Nay, I bear it

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: on my shoulders as a beggar wont her brat, and I

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: think when he hath lamed me, I shall beg with it

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: from door to door.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Come, go along. My wife is coming yonder.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Mistress, respice finem, respect

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: your end, or rather, the prophecy like the parrot,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: “Beware the rope’s end.”

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Wilt thou still talk?

speaker: COURTESAN
text: How say you now? Is not your husband mad?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: His incivility confirms no less.—

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Good Doctor Pinch, you are a conjurer;

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Establish him in his true sense again,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And I will please you what you will demand.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Alas, how fiery and how sharp he looks!

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Mark how he trembles in his ecstasy.

speaker: PINCH
text: Give me your hand, and let me feel your pulse.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: There is my hand, and let it feel your ear.

speaker: PINCH
text: I charge thee, Satan, housed within this man,

speaker: PINCH
text: To yield possession to my holy prayers,

speaker: PINCH
text: And to thy state of darkness hie thee straight.

speaker: PINCH
text: I conjure thee by all the saints in heaven.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Peace, doting wizard, peace. I am not mad.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: O, that thou wert not, poor distressèd soul!

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: You minion, you, are these your customers?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Did this companion with the saffron face

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Revel and feast it at my house today

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Whilst upon me the guilty doors were shut

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And I denied to enter in my house?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: O husband, God doth know you dined at home,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Where would you had remained until this time,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Free from these slanders and this open shame.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text:
“Dined at home”?
                 
                To
                 
                Dromio
                .
               Thou villain, what sayest thou?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Sir, sooth to say, you did not dine at home.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Were not my doors locked up and I shut out?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Perdie, your doors were locked, and you shut out.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And did not she herself revile me there?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Sans fable, she herself reviled you there.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Did not her kitchen maid rail, taunt, and scorn me?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Certes, she did; the kitchen vestal scorned you.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And did not I in rage depart from thence?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: In verity you did.—My bones bears witness,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: That since have felt the vigor of his rage.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Is ’t good to soothe him in these contraries?

speaker: PINCH
text: It is no shame. The fellow finds his vein

speaker: PINCH
text: And, yielding to him, humors well his frenzy.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Thou hast suborned the goldsmith to arrest me.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Alas, I sent you money to redeem you

speaker: ADRIANA
text: By Dromio here, who came in haste for it.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Money by me? Heart and goodwill you might,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: But surely, master, not a rag of money.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Went’st not thou to her for a purse of ducats?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: He came to me, and I delivered it.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: And I am witness with her that she did.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: God and the rope-maker bear me witness

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: That I was sent for nothing but a rope.

speaker: PINCH
text: Mistress, both man and master is possessed.

speaker: PINCH
text: I know it by their pale and deadly looks.

speaker: PINCH
text: They must be bound and laid in some dark room.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Say wherefore didst thou lock me forth today.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text:
To
                 
                Dromio
                 
                of
                 
                Ephesus
                .
                 
              And why dost thou deny the bag of gold?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I did not, gentle husband, lock thee forth.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And, gentle master, I received no gold.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: But I confess, sir, that we were locked out.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Dissembling villain, thou speak’st false in both.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Dissembling harlot, thou art false in all,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And art confederate with a damnèd pack

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: To make a loathsome abject scorn of me.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: But with these nails I’ll pluck out these false eyes

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: That would behold in me this shameful sport.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: O bind him, bind him! Let him not come near me.

speaker: PINCH
text: More company! The fiend is strong within him.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Ay me, poor man, how pale and wan he looks!

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: What, will you murder me?—Thou jailer, thou,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I am thy prisoner. Wilt thou suffer them

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: To make a rescue?

speaker: OFFICER
text: Masters, let him go.

speaker: OFFICER
text: He is my prisoner, and you shall not have him.

speaker: PINCH
text: Go, bind this man, for he is frantic too.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: What wilt thou do, thou peevish officer?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Hast thou delight to see a wretched man

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Do outrage and displeasure to himself?

speaker: OFFICER
text: He is my prisoner. If I let him go,

speaker: OFFICER
text: The debt he owes will be required of me.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I will discharge thee ere I go from thee.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Bear me forthwith unto his creditor,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And knowing how the debt grows, I will pay it.—

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Good Master Doctor, see him safe conveyed

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Home to my house. O most unhappy day!

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: O most unhappy strumpet!

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Master, I am here entered in bond for you.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Out on thee, villain! Wherefore dost thou mad me?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Will you be bound for nothing? Be mad, good master.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Cry “The devil!”

speaker: LUCIANA
text: God help poor souls! How idly do they talk!

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Go bear him hence.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Sister, go you with me.

speaker: ADRIANA
text:
To
                 
                Officer
                .
                 
              Say now whose suit is he arrested at.

speaker: OFFICER
text: One Angelo, a goldsmith. Do you know him?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I know the man. What is the sum he owes?

speaker: OFFICER
text: Two hundred ducats.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Say, how grows it due?

speaker: OFFICER
text: Due for a chain your husband had of him.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: He did bespeak a chain for me but had it not.

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Whenas your husband all in rage today

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Came to my house and took away my ring,

speaker: COURTESAN
text: The ring I saw upon his finger now,

speaker: COURTESAN
text: Straight after did I meet him with a chain.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: It may be so, but I did never see it.—

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Come, jailer, bring me where the goldsmith is.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I long to know the truth hereof at large.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: God for Thy mercy, they are loose again!

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And come with naked swords. Let’s call more help

speaker: ADRIANA
text: To have them bound again.

speaker: OFFICER
text: Away! They’ll kill us.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I see these witches are afraid of swords.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: She that would be your wife now ran from you.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Come to the Centaur. Fetch our stuff from thence.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I long that we were safe and sound aboard.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Faith, stay here this night. They

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: will surely do us no harm. You saw they speak us

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: fair, give us gold. Methinks they are such a gentle

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: nation that, but for the mountain of mad flesh that

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: claims marriage of me, I could find in my heart to

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: stay here still, and turn witch.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I will not stay tonight for all the town.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Therefore, away, to get our stuff aboard.
